{{Infobox concept
| name = World Spirit
| introduced = The Captain
}}

''See also: [[World Spirit Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[World Spirits]] are non-biological manifestations of [[Aether]] that represent fundamental concepts or planetary identities. Rather than being physical beings, they function similarly to digital avatars; while they can project themselves into reality in various forms—ranging from human-like figures to massive entities composed of earth and stone—their true existence remains beyond mortal influence. These spirits are intrinsically linked to the Aetheric flows of their respective domains, such as planets or ships. On habitable worlds, a World Spirit is essential for maintaining a livable atmosphere and protecting the ecosystem. In some instances, powerful spirits can extend their influence across entire star systems, acting as protectors for all inhabitants within that system. Because their consciousness is spread across their entire domain, interacting with them can be difficult, often requiring specific magnification rituals to capture their attention. Spirits typically reflect the nature of the environment they inhabit; for example, those on harsh worlds may be more independent or difficult to negotiate with. They possess immense power derived from their core nature, such as [[life]] or water, which can be used for large-scale magical amplification. However, they are also vulnerable; killing a spirit can lead to the rapid collapse of a planet's ecosystem. While most are tied to celestial bodies, some can be bound to objects like ships or [[Zenith Devices]].}}

== The Captain ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A [[World Spirit]] is required to sustain a livable atmosphere on a planet.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Horizon]] identifies as a [[World Spirit]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45">''The Captain'', chapter 45</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Engineer ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Zenith Starship]] has a holographic [[World Spirit]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c3">''The Engineer'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|A person could be possessed by a [[World Spirit]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c4">''The Engineer'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[World Spirits]] can be called up and have personalities based on their reflections in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[World Spirits]] are entities capable of being engaged in bargains. While [[Beshkin]] possesses a World Spirit characterized by its neutrality, the moon of [[Titan Force headquarters]] lacks one entirely. The narrator utilizes a World Spirit to assist with the process of casting. During a confrontation with [[Neth’terith]], this spirit defends by employing green shields and discharging lightning blasts.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[World Spirits]] are entities that can be bargained with.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c11">''The Knight'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator has a [[World Spirit]] to help with casting.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18">''The Knight'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[World Spirit]] resists [[Neth’terith]] with lightning blasts and green shields.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20">''The Knight'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Beshkin]] has a [[World Spirit]] known for its neutrality.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c21">''The Knight'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The moon of [[Titan Force headquarters]] does not have its own [[World Spirit]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|One of approximately a million spirits existing across various planets, the World Spirit possesses a chaotic and unreliable nature. Driven by an eagerness to alter the development of [[Basyrryx]], this entity has the ability to manifest itself to provide information. During a pivotal moment, it clasped hands with the narrator to finalize a deal. However, this interaction led to the narrator casting a sealing spell against the spirit, marking the first time the narrator had ever cursed such an entity.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">7</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[World Spirit]] was eager to change the development of [[Basyrryx]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c7">''The Pilot'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|About a million planets have [[World Spirits]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c10">''The Pilot'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[World Spirit]] is described as chaotic and unreliable.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[World Spirit]] can manifest and provide information.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c31">''The Pilot'', chapter 31</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[World Spirit]] clasped hands with the narrator to seal a deal.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c48">''The Pilot'', chapter 48</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[World Spirit]] was targeted with a sealing spell.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c49">''The Pilot'', chapter 49</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The narrator has never cursed a [[World Spirit]] before.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c49" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon concepts]]
[[Category:Lifeforms]]
